metadata.description: SmartPatch Service Controls — production-grade schema (SVC-01 .. SVC-08). Each control contains enforcement commands, validation checks, failure modes, privileges and authoritative references. Added WinRM/Defender/RDP service controls based on scope and defensive criteria.
metadata.author: SmartPatch Team (converted)

controls.name: Disable Remote Registry service
controls.description: Disable the Remote Registry service so registry cannot be read/modified remotely.
controls.rationale: Remote Registry exposes registry over the network and increases lateral information-disclosure and modification surface.

os_applicability.notes: Ensure alternate remote management (WinRM/agent) before disabling.

commands: reg add "HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\RemoteRegistry" /v Start /t REG_DWORD /d 4 /f
sc stop RemoteRegistry
enforcement_methods.notes: Start = 4 sets Disabled. Use GPO service security templates for enterprise rollout.

validation_checks.command: reg query "HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\RemoteRegistry" /v Start & sc query RemoteRegistry
validation_checks.success_criteria: Start = 4 and service state = STOPPED or Disabled

failure_modes: Remote management/monitoring tooling that relies on RemoteRegistry will fail; ensure alternatives (SCCM/WinRM/agent) are available.

priority.priority_reason: Removes a remote attack surface; validate management alternatives.

references.title: How to disable Windows Remote Registry? (MS Docs / Q&A)

controls.implementation_notes: Pilot on non-critical hosts and confirm remote-management replacement. Deploy via GPO service templates where possible.

controls.name: Disable Telnet client and server
controls.description: Remove/disable Telnet server/client to eliminate insecure cleartext remote access.
controls.rationale: Telnet transmits credentials in cleartext; modern environments must use SSH or secure management channels.

os_applicability.notes: TelnetClient is an optional Windows feature; Telnet server may not be installed by default.

commands: dism /online /Disable-Feature /FeatureName:TelnetClient
sc config TlntSvr start= disabled & sc stop TlntSvr  (if Telnet Server present)
enforcement_methods.notes: Use DISM or Windows Features to remove TelnetClient; disable Telnet server service if present.

validation_checks.success_criteria: Telnet service absent or Disabled and TelnetClient feature Disabled/NotPresent

failure_modes: Legacy automation or third-party appliances that require Telnet will fail — provide SSH-based alternatives or isolated legacy VLANs.

priority.priority_reason: Telnet is insecure and should be removed in all modern environments.

references.title: How to enable/disable Telnet on Windows (feature removal guidance)

controls.implementation_notes: Replace automation that uses Telnet with SSH; include exception handling for irreducible legacy systems.

controls.name: Disable Print Spooler where not required
controls.description: Stop and disable Print Spooler (Spooler) on systems that do not require printing (especially Domain Controllers).
controls.rationale: Print Spooler has been exploited (PrintNightmare) and Microsoft / CISA recommend disabling it on high-risk systems that do not require printing.

os_applicability.notes: Domain Controllers and critical servers advised to disable Spooler unless printing required.

commands: sc stop Spooler & sc config Spooler start= disabled
Or use GPO: Computer Configuration → Policies → Windows Settings → Security Settings → System Services → Print Spooler = Disabled
enforcement_methods.notes: Stopping Spooler reduces exposure; Microsoft guidance recommends disabling on Domain Controllers and other non-printing servers during elevated risk.

validation_checks.success_criteria: Service status = Stopped and startup type = Disabled

failure_modes: Print functionality will be lost on affected hosts; ensure business justification documented and alternate printing routes available if needed.
controls.functional_impact: High on systems that require printing; Low on headless servers.

priority.priority_reason: Print Spooler has had critical remote-execution vulnerabilities; disabling reduces attack surface.

references.title: Microsoft guidance on Print Spooler and PrintNightmare (MSRC)
CISA advisory recommending disabling Print Spooler on Domain Controllers

controls.implementation_notes: Prioritize Domain Controllers and servers that do not print. Use maintenance windows and pilot groups; document rollback steps to re-enable if needed.

controls.name: Harden Windows Update / Windows Update service configuration
controls.description: Ensure Windows Update service and related settings are configured to apply security updates per organizational policy and prevent unauthorized disabling.
controls.rationale: Timely application of security updates reduces exposure to known vulnerabilities; Windows Update settings must be consistent and protected from unauthorized change.

os_applicability.notes: Group Policy and WSUS/Configuration Manager preferred for enterprise control.

commands: GPO path: Computer Configuration → Administrative Templates → Windows Components → Windows Update → Configure Automatic Updates (set per policy)
Use WSUS/SCCM/Intune to control update source and scheduling.
enforcement_methods.notes: Avoid setting NoAutoUpdate unless using centralized update management; prefer forced patching/scheduling and auditability.

commands: reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsUpdate\AU" /v NoAutoUpdate /t REG_DWORD /d 0 /f
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsUpdate\AU" /v AUOptions /t REG_DWORD /d 4 /f
enforcement_methods.notes: AUOptions values control behavior; use documented values for auto-download/install schedule per environment.

validation_checks.success_criteria: Policy keys reflect intended update configuration and source (WSUS) and NoAutoUpdate not set to disable updates unless intended.
Windows Update service present and running per expected policy (may be manual if managed).

failure_modes: Misconfiguration can disable updates or cause surge activity during rollout; ensure phased deployment and bandwidth considerations.

priority.priority_reason: Patch management is the strongest systemic mitigation against known-vulnerability exploitation.

references.title: Configure Automatic Updates and Windows Update settings (Microsoft Docs)

controls.implementation_notes: Use WSUS/SCCM/Intune for centralized control and reporting. Do not leave systems with NoAutoUpdate=1 unless part of documented exception process.

controls.name: Disable unused legacy services (inventory-driven)
controls.description: Identify and disable unused legacy services (e.g., SNMP, TFTP, Simple TCP/IP Services) following inventory and risk assessment.
controls.rationale: Unused legacy services increase attack surface; disable them where business functions do not require them.

os_applicability.notes: Service availability varies by OS and installed roles/features.

commands: sc config <ServiceName> start= disabled & sc stop <ServiceName>
Example: sc config SNMP start= disabled & sc stop SNMP
enforcement_methods.notes: Use Security Templates / GPO to enforce services state at scale; document exceptions.

validation_checks.success_criteria: Target legacy services set to Disabled/Stopped per inventory plan

failure_modes: Disabling services without inventory can break business functions; perform asset owners sign-off and staged rollout.

priority.priority_reason: Removes low-value attack surface; must be inventory-driven.

audit_query_examples: Compare service states to inventory baseline

references.title: Security guidelines for disabling system services (Microsoft Docs)
Configure SNMP Service (MS guidance) — inventory and secure if required

controls.implementation_notes: Create an inventory of service dependencies, pilot disable on small groups, and provide rollback/playbook for impacted systems.

controls.name: Ensure antimalware service (Microsoft Defender) present and running
controls.description: Ensure antimalware service (WinDefend / MsMpEng) is enabled and running, or that an approved enterprise AV/EDR agent is present and healthy.
controls.rationale: Active endpoint protection reduces exploitation, detection and remediation time; absence increases risk of undetected compromise.

os_applicability.notes: On platforms where Microsoft Defender not primary, ensure enterprise AV is installed and reporting.

commands: Get-Service -Name WinDefend; sc queryex WinDefend
If absent, deploy approved AV/EDR agent via endpoint management
enforcement_methods.notes: Do not run multiple real-time AV engines concurrently unless explicitly supported; follow vendor guidance.

validation_checks.success_criteria: Service running and/or EDR agent present and healthy per vendor telemetry

validation_checks.command: Check endpoint management console for healthy agent heartbeat
validation_checks.success_criteria: Agent reporting OK to central console

failure_modes: Conflicts between multiple AV engines may cause performance issues; vendor guidance required for coexistence or exclusions.
High CPU from MsMpEng may require tuning/exclusions for large file servers.

priority.priority_reason: Endpoint protection is a core prevention/detection control.

audit_query_examples: Verify AV/EDR heartbeat in central management

references.title: Troubleshoot Microsoft Defender for Endpoint performance and service behavior (MS Docs)

controls.implementation_notes: Ensure approved AV/EDR is deployed and reporting before disabling other agents. Tune exclusions for high-throughput servers per vendor guidance.

controls.name: Harden WinRM (Windows Remote Management) listeners and authentication
controls.description: Configure WinRM listeners for HTTPS, restrict allowed users, and enforce Kerberos/NTLMv2; disable HTTP listeners where not required.
controls.rationale: WinRM provides powerful remote management; insecure configuration (HTTP listener, weak auth) increases risk of credential theft and lateral movement.

os_applicability.notes: WinRM versions vary; prefer HTTPS listeners and certificate-based authentication for cross-domain or internet-exposed management.

commands: winrm quickconfig -transport:https
winrm create winrm/config/Listener?Address=*+Transport=HTTPS @{Hostname="<fqdn>";CertificateThumbprint="<thumbprint>"}
Set-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName 'WinRM HTTPS' -RemoteAddress <trusted-range> -Action Allow
enforcement_methods.notes: Use certificate issued by trusted CA for HTTPS listener and restrict remote addresses via firewall. Disable HTTP listener if not needed.

commands: Configure WinRM service ACLs and use Group Policy to limit which users/groups can use WinRM.
enforcement_methods.notes: Use GPO: Computer Configuration → Administrative Templates → Windows Components → Windows Remote Management.

validation_checks.success_criteria: Only expected HTTPS listeners present; HTTP listener absent or disabled
Successful connection over HTTPS from trusted management host and failure from untrusted host

failure_modes: Misconfigured certificates or firewall rules can block management; test in lab before mass rollout.
Legacy management scripts expecting HTTP WinRM may fail and need updating.
controls.functional_impact: Medium during rollout (management tooling may need updates)

priority.priority_reason: Secure remote management reduces credential exposure and lateral movement risk.

references.title: WinRM installation and configuration guidance (MS Docs)
Operational guidance for configuring WinRM HTTPS listeners (practical notes)

controls.implementation_notes: Deploy certificate infrastructure for HTTPS listeners, restrict RemoteAddress via firewall, and roll out in pilot groups. Provide fall-back console access for management if misconfiguration occurs.

controls.name: Disable Remote Desktop Services (TermService) where not required
controls.description: Stop and disable Remote Desktop Services (TermService) on endpoints that do not require Remote Desktop functionality.
controls.rationale: Unneeded RDP increases attack surface; where RDP is not required, disabling the service reduces exposure and aligns with network-level controls restricting RDP.

os_applicability.notes: Consider application servers and admin hosts where RDP may be needed; coordinate with admin teams.

commands: sc stop TermService & sc config TermService start= disabled
Or use GPO: Computer Configuration → Policies → Administrative Templates → Windows Components → Remote Desktop Services → Do not allow remote connections (or fDenyTSConnections registry)
enforcement_methods.notes: Setting fDenyTSConnections = 1 under HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Terminal Server denies new RDP connections; disabling TermService prevents RDS functionality completely.

validation_checks.command: Get-Service TermService -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; reg query "HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Terminal Server" /v fDenyTSConnections
validation_checks.success_criteria: Service stopped/Disabled and fDenyTSConnections = 1 (if deny approach used)

failure_modes: Remote administrators may lose remote console access; ensure VPN/RD Gateway or console access is available as a break-glass.
Some management tooling may rely on RDP; plan exceptions and documented processes.

priority.priority_reason: Reduces a common external and lateral movement vector.

references.title: Disable or secure Remote Desktop Services — Microsoft Docs / GPO guidance
CISA guidance to disable RDP where not required

controls.implementation_notes: Prefer network-layer restrictions (allow-list + RD Gateway + MFA) over outright disabling where administrative needs exist; apply conservative pilot rollout.

source_references_loadbearing.claim: Remote Registry can be disabled via service Start registry and stopped; remote management alternatives recommended.
Telnet is an optional Windows feature and should be removed; use DISM/Features to disable.
Print Spooler has been exploited (PrintNightmare); MS/CISA guidance recommend disabling on DCs and non-printing servers.
Windows Update should be centrally managed (WSUS/SCCM/Intune/GPO) and not disabled inadvertently.
Security guidance exists for safely disabling system services; inventory and testing required.
WinRM should be secured with HTTPS listeners and restricted to trusted management hosts.
Ensure antimalware service is present and healthy; tune per vendor guidance.
Disable/limit RDP where not needed and prefer RD Gateway/VPN + MFA for remote access.
